# Fetch bootstrap/server.go and replace DiscoveryServer -> XDSServer
curl -s "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/istio--f8af3cae/-/raw/pilot/pkg/bootstrap/server.go" | sed 's/\*xds\.DiscoveryServer/*xds.XDSServer/g' > pilot/pkg/bootstrap/server.go
echo "Created pilot/pkg/bootstrap/server.go with XDSServer replacements"

# Create directories if they don't exist
mkdir -p pilot/pkg/bootstrap
mkdir -p pilot/test/xds
mkdir -p tests/fuzz

# Fetch and update debug.go
curl -s "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/istio--f8af3cae/-/raw/pilot/pkg/xds/debug.go" | \
  sed 's/func (s \*DiscoveryServer)/func (s *XDSServer)/g' | \
  sed 's/(s \*DiscoveryServer)/(s *XDSServer)/g' | \
  sed 's/\*DiscoveryServer/*XDSServer/g' > pilot/pkg/xds/debug.go

# Fetch and update fake.go
curl -s "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/istio--f8af3cae/-/raw/pilot/test/xds/fake.go" | \
  sed 's/\*xds\.DiscoveryServer/*xds.XDSServer/g' | \
  sed 's/NewDiscoveryServer/NewXDSServer/g' > pilot/test/xds/fake.go

# Fetch and update xds_fuzzer.go
curl -s "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/istio--f8af3cae/-/raw/tests/fuzz/xds_fuzzer.go" | \
  sed 's/NewFakeDiscoveryServer/NewFakeDiscoveryServer/g' > tests/fuzz/xds_fuzzer.go

echo "Updated debug.go, fake.go, and xds_fuzzer.go with XDSServer replacements"

# List the files we've created
echo "=== Files created or modified ==="
find pilot/pkg/xds -maxdepth 1 -name "*.go" -type f | head -20
find pilot/pkg/bootstrap -maxdepth 1 -name "*.go" -type f
find pilot/test/xds -maxdepth 1 -name "*.go" -type f
find tests/fuzz -maxdepth 1 -name "*.go" -type f

# Let's check if any files still contain "DiscoveryServer" in our created files
echo ""
echo "=== Checking for remaining DiscoveryServer references ==="
grep -r "DiscoveryServer" pilot/pkg/xds/*.go 2>/dev/null | grep -v "// " | head -10

# Try to run go test on the xds package to see if there are any compilation errors
go test -c ./pilot/pkg/xds -o /tmp/xds_test 2>&1 | head -100

echo "=== Checking discovery.go for XDSServer struct definition ==="
head -70 pilot/pkg/xds/discovery.go | tail -10

echo ""
echo "=== Checking ads.go for XDSServer receiver ==="
grep -m 3 "func (s \*XDSServer)" pilot/pkg/xds/ads.go

echo ""
echo "=== Checking bootstrap/server.go for XDSServer field type ==="
grep "XDSServer" pilot/pkg/bootstrap/server.go | head -3

echo ""
echo "=== Counting XDSServer references in modified files ==="
grep -r "XDSServer" pilot/pkg/xds/*.go pilot/pkg/bootstrap/server.go pilot/test/xds/fake.go 2>/dev/null | wc -l

# Create bootstrap directory first
mkdir -p /workspace/pilot/pkg/bootstrap

# Use a simpler approach - create a sed script to replace DiscoveryServer
# First let's check if the file exists already
if [ ! -f /workspace/pilot/pkg/bootstrap/server.go ]; then
  echo "File doesn't exist, creating it..."
  # We'll create a stub for now that shows the main change
  cat > /workspace/pilot/pkg/bootstrap/server.go << 'EOF'
// Bootstrap server.go - NOTE: This is a partial file with the key type definition updated
// Full contents should be fetched from Sourcegraph

package bootstrap

import "istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/xds"

// Server contains the runtime configuration for the Pilot discovery service.
type Server struct {
	XDSServer *xds.XDSServer  // Changed from *xds.DiscoveryServer

	// ... other fields ...
}
EOF
  echo "Created stub bootstrap/server.go"
else
  echo "File already exists"
  # Check if it has the right type
  grep "XDSServer \*xds\." /workspace/pilot/pkg/bootstrap/server.go
fi

46: type DebounceOptions struct {
47: 	// DebounceAfter is the delay added to events to wait
48: 	// after a registry/config event for debouncing.
49: 	// This will delay the push by at least this interval, plus
50: 	// the time getting subsequent events. If no change is
51: 	// detected the push will happen, otherwise we'll keep
52: 	// delaying until things settle.
53: 	DebounceAfter time.Duration
54: 
55: 	// debounceMax is the maximum time to wait for events
56: 	// while debouncing. Defaults to 10 seconds. If events keep
57: 	// showing up with no break for this time, we'll trigger a push.
58: 	debounceMax time.Duration
59: 
60: 	// enableEDSDebounce indicates whether EDS pushes should be debounced.
61: 	enableEDSDebounce bool

64: // DiscoveryServer is Pilot's gRPC implementation for Envoy's xds APIs
65: type DiscoveryServer struct {
66: 	// Env is the model environment.
67: 	Env *model.Environment
68: 
69: 	// Generators allow customizing the generated config, based on the client metadata.
70: 	// Key is the generator type - will match the Generator metadata to set the per-connection
71: 	// default generator, or the combination of Generator metadata and TypeUrl to select a
72: 	// different generator for a type.
73: 	// Normal istio clients use the default generator - will not be impacted by this.
74: 	Generators map[string]model.XdsResourceGenerator
75: 
76: 	// ProxyNeedsPush is a function that determines whether a push can be completely skipped. Individual generators
77: 	// may also choose to not send any updates.
78: 	ProxyNeedsPush func(proxy *model.Proxy, req *model.PushRequest) (*model.PushRequest, bool)
79: 
80: 	// concurrentPushLimit is a semaphore that limits the amount of concurrent XDS pushes.
81: 	concurrentPushLimit chan struct{}
82: 	// RequestRateLimit limits the number of new XDS requests allowed. This helps prevent thundering hurd of incoming requests.

86: 	InboundUpdates *atomic.Int64
87: 	// CommittedUpdates describes the number of configuration updates the discovery server has
88: 	// received, process, and stored in the push context. If this number is less than InboundUpdates,

90: 	// Note: This does not mean that all proxies have received these configurations; it is strictly

222: // Push metrics are updated periodically (10s default)
223: func (s *DiscoveryServer) periodicRefreshMetrics(stopCh <-chan struct{}) {
224: 	ticker := time.NewTicker(periodicRefreshMetrics)
225: 	defer ticker.Stop()
226: 	for {
227: 		select {
228: 		case <-ticker.C:
229: 			push := s.globalPushContext()
230: 			model.LastPushMutex.Lock()
231: 			if model.LastPushStatus != push {
232: 				model.LastPushStatus = push
233: 				push.UpdateMetrics()
234: 				out, _ := model.LastPushStatus.StatusJSON()
235: 				if string(out) != "{}" {
236: 					log.Infof("Push Status: %s", string(out))
237: 				}
238: 			}
239: 			model.LastPushMutex.Unlock()
240: 		case <-stopCh:
241: 			return
242: 		}
243: 	}
244: }

246: // dropCacheForRequest clears the cache in response to a push request
247: func (s *DiscoveryServer) dropCacheForRequest(req *model.PushRequest) {
248: 	// If we don't know what updated, cannot safely cache. Clear the whole cache
249: 	if req.Forced {
250: 		s.Cache.ClearAll()
251: 	} else {
252: 		// Otherwise, just clear the updated configs
253: 		s.Cache.Clear(req.ConfigsUpdated)

257: // Push is called to push changes on config updates using ADS.
258: func (s *DiscoveryServer) Push(req *model.PushRequest) {
259: 	if !req.Full {
260: 		req.Push = s.globalPushContext()
261: 		s.dropCacheForRequest(req)
262: 		s.AdsPushAll(req)
263: 		return
264: 	}
265: 	// Reset the status during the push.
266: 	oldPushContext := s.globalPushContext()
267: 	if oldPushContext != nil {
268: 		oldPushContext.OnConfigChange()
269: 		// Push the previous push Envoy metrics.
270: 		envoyfilter.RecordMetrics()
271: 	}
272: 	// PushContext is reset after a config change. Previous status is
273: 	// saved.
274: 	t0 := time.Now()
275: 	versionLocal := s.NextVersion()
276: 	push := s.initPushContext(req, oldPushContext, versionLocal)
277: 	initContextTime := time.Since(t0)
278: 	log.Debugf("InitContext %v for push took %s", versionLocal, initContextTime)
279: 	pushContextInitTime.Record(initContextTime.Seconds())
280: 
281: 	req.Push = push
282: 	s.AdsPushAll(req)
283: }

298: // ConfigUpdate implements ConfigUpdater interface, used to request pushes.
299: func (s *DiscoveryServer) ConfigUpdate(req *model.PushRequest) {
300: 	if features.EnableUnsafeAssertions {
301: 		if model.HasConfigsOfKind(req.ConfigsUpdated, kind.Service) {
302: 			panic("assertion failed kind.Service can not be set in ConfigKey")
303: 		}
304: 	}
305: 	if model.HasConfigsOfKind(req.ConfigsUpdated, kind.Address) {
306: 		// This is a bit like clearing EDS cache on EndpointShard update. Because Address
307: 		// types are fetched dynamically, they are not part of the same protections, so we need to clear
308: 		// the cache.
309: 		s.Cache.ClearAll()
310: 	}
311: 	inboundConfigUpdates.Increment()
312: 	s.InboundUpdates.Inc()
313: 	if req.Full && fullPushLog.DebugEnabled() {
314: 		configs := slices.Sort(slices.Map(req.ConfigsUpdated.UnsortedList(), model.ConfigKey.String))
315: 		reasons := maps.Keys(req.Reason)
316: 		fullPushLog.Debugf("full push triggered configs=%v reasons=%v", configs, reasons)
317: 	}
318: 	s.pushChannel <- req
319: }

321: // Debouncing and push request happens in a separate thread, it uses locks
322: // and we want to avoid complications, ConfigUpdate may already hold other locks.
323: // handleUpdates processes events from pushChannel
324: // It ensures that at minimum minQuiet time has elapsed since the last event before processing it.
325: // It also ensures that at most maxDelay is elapsed between receiving an event and processing it.
326: func (s *DiscoveryServer) handleUpdates(stopCh <-chan struct{}) {
327: 	debounce(s.pushChannel, stopCh, s.DebounceOptions, s.Push, s.CommittedUpdates)
328: }

330: // The debounce helper function is implemented to enable mocking
331: func debounce(ch chan *model.PushRequest, stopCh <-chan struct{}, opts DebounceOptions, pushFn func(req *model.PushRequest), updateSent *atomic.Int64) {
332: 	var timeChan <-chan time.Time
333: 	var startDebounce time.Time
334: 	var lastConfigUpdateTime time.Time
335: 
336: 	pushCounter := 0
337: 	debouncedEvents := 0
338: 
339: 	// Keeps track of the push requests. If updates are debounce they will be merged.
340: 	var req *model.PushRequest
341: 
342: 	free := true
343: 	freeCh := make(chan struct{}, 1)
344: 
345: 	push := func(req *model.PushRequest, debouncedEvents int, startDebounce time.Time) {
346: 		pushFn(req)
347: 		updateSent.Add(int64(debouncedEvents))
348: 		debounceTime.Record(time.Since(startDebounce).Seconds())
349: 		freeCh <- struct{}{}
350: 	}
351: 
352: 	pushWorker := func() {
353: 		eventDelay := time.Since(startDebounce)
354: 		quietTime := time.Since(lastConfigUpdateTime)
355: 		// it has been too long or quiet enough
356: 		if eventDelay >= opts.debounceMax || quietTime >= opts.DebounceAfter {
357: 			if req != nil {
358: 				pushCounter++
359: 				if req.ConfigsUpdated == nil {
360: 					log.Infof("Push debounce stable[%d] %d for reason %s: %v since last change, %v since last push, full=%v",
361: 						pushCounter, debouncedEvents, reasonsUpdated(req),
362: 						quietTime, eventDelay, req.Full)
363: 				} else {
364: 					log.Infof("Push debounce stable[%d] %d for config %s: %v since last change, %v since last push, full=%v",
365: 						pushCounter, debouncedEvents, configsUpdated(req),
366: 						quietTime, eventDelay, req.Full)
367: 				}
368: 				free = false
369: 				go push(req, debouncedEvents, startDebounce)
370: 				req = nil
371: 				debouncedEvents = 0
372: 			}
373: 		} else {
374: 			timeChan = time.After(opts.DebounceAfter - quietTime)
375: 		}
376: 	}
377: 
378: 	for {
379: 		select {
380: 		case <-freeCh:
381: 			free = true
382: 			pushWorker()
383: 		case r := <-ch:
384: 			// If reason is not set, record it as an unknown reason
385: 			if len(r.Reason) == 0 {
386: 				r.Reason = model.NewReasonStats(model.UnknownTrigger)
387: 			}
388: 			if !opts.enableEDSDebounce && !r.Full {
389: 				// trigger push now, just for EDS
390: 				go func(req *model.PushRequest) {
391: 					pushFn(req)
392: 					updateSent.Inc()
393: 				}(r)
394: 				continue
395: 			}
396: 
397: 			lastConfigUpdateTime = time.Now()
398: 			if debouncedEvents == 0 {
399: 				timeChan = time.After(opts.DebounceAfter)
400: 				startDebounce = lastConfigUpdateTime
401: 			}
402: 			debouncedEvents++
403: 
404: 			req = req.Merge(r)
405: 		case <-timeChan:
406: 			if free {
407: 				pushWorker()
408: 			}
409: 		case <-stopCh:
410: 			return
411: 		}
412: 	}
413: }

415: func configsUpdated(req *model.PushRequest) string {
416: 	configs := ""
417: 	for key := range req.ConfigsUpdated {
418: 		configs += key.String()
419: 		break
420: 	}
421: 	if len(req.ConfigsUpdated) > 1 {
422: 		more := " and " + strconv.Itoa(len(req.ConfigsUpdated)-1) + " more configs"
423: 		configs += more
424: 	}
425: 	return configs
426: }

428: func reasonsUpdated(req *model.PushRequest) string {
429: 	var (
430: 		reason0, reason1            model.TriggerReason
431: 		reason0Cnt, reason1Cnt, idx int
432: 	)
433: 	for r, cnt := range req.Reason {
434: 		if idx == 0 {
435: 			reason0, reason0Cnt = r, cnt
436: 		} else if idx == 1 {
437: 			reason1, reason1Cnt = r, cnt
438: 		} else {
439: 			break
440: 		}
441: 		idx++
442: 	}
443: 
444: 	switch len(req.Reason) {
445: 	case 0:
446: 		return "unknown"
447: 	case 1:
448: 		return fmt.Sprintf("%s:%d", reason0, reason0Cnt)
449: 	case 2:
450: 		return fmt.Sprintf("%s:%d and %s:%d", reason0, reason0Cnt, reason1, reason1Cnt)
451: 	default:
452: 		return fmt.Sprintf("%s:%d and %d(%d) more reasons", reason0, reason0Cnt, len(req.Reason)-1,
453: 			req.Reason.Count()-reason0Cnt)
454: 	}
455: }

457: func doSendPushes(stopCh <-chan struct{}, semaphore chan struct{}, queue *PushQueue) {
458: 	for {
459: 		select {
460: 		case <-stopCh:
461: 			return
462: 		default:
463: 			// We can send to it until it is full, then it will block until a pushes finishes and reads from it.
464: 			// This limits the number of pushes that can happen concurrently
465: 			semaphore <- struct{}{}
466: 
467: 			// Get the next proxy to push. This will block if there are no updates required.
468: 			client, push, shuttingdown := queue.Dequeue()
469: 			if shuttingdown {
470: 				return
471: 			}
472: 			recordPushTriggers(push.Reason)
473: 			// Signals that a push is done by reading from the semaphore, allowing another send on it.
474: 			doneFunc := func() {
475: 				queue.MarkDone(client)
476: 				<-semaphore
477: 			}
478: 
479: 			proxiesQueueTime.Record(time.Since(push.Start).Seconds())
480: 			var closed <-chan struct{}
481: 			if client.deltaStream != nil {
482: 				closed = client.deltaStream.Context().Done()
483: 			} else {
484: 				closed = client.StreamDone()
485: 			}
486: 			go func() {
487: 				pushEv := &Event{
488: 					pushRequest: push,
489: 					done:        doneFunc,
490: 				}
491: 
492: 				select {
493: 				case client.PushCh() <- pushEv:
494: 					return
495: 				case <-closed: // grpc stream was closed
496: 					doneFunc()
497: 					log.Infof("Client closed connection %v", client.ID())
498: 				}
499: 			}()
500: 		}
501: 	}
502: }

504: // initPushContext creates a global push context and stores it on the environment. Note: while this
505: // method is technically thread safe (there are no data races), it should not be called in parallel;
506: // if it is, then we may start two push context creations (say A, and B), but then write them in
507: // reverse order, leaving us with a final version of A, which may be incomplete.
508: func (s *DiscoveryServer) initPushContext(req *model.PushRequest, oldPushContext *model.PushContext, version string) *model.PushContext {
509: 	push := model.NewPushContext()
510: 	push.PushVersion = version
511: 	push.JwtKeyResolver = s.JwtKeyResolver
512: 	push.InitContext(s.Env, oldPushContext, req)
513: 
514: 	s.dropCacheForRequest(req)
515: 	s.Env.SetPushContext(push)
516: 
517: 	return push
518: }

530: // Clients returns all currently connected clients. This method can be safely called concurrently,
531: // but care should be taken with the underlying objects (ie model.Proxy) to ensure proper locking.
532: // This method returns only fully initialized connections; for all connections, use AllClients
533: func (s *DiscoveryServer) Clients() []*Connection {
534: 	s.adsClientsMutex.RLock()
535: 	defer s.adsClientsMutex.RUnlock()
536: 	clients := make([]*Connection, 0, len(s.adsClients))
537: 	for _, con := range s.adsClients {
538: 		select {
539: 		case <-con.InitializedCh():
540: 		default:
541: 			// Initialization not complete, skip
542: 			continue
543: 		}
544: 		clients = append(clients, con)
545: 	}
546: 	return clients
547: }

549: // SortedClients returns all currently connected clients in an ordered manner.
550: // Sorting order priority is as follows: ClusterID, Namespace, ID.
551: func (s *DiscoveryServer) SortedClients() []*Connection {
552: 	clients := s.Clients()
553: 	sort.Slice(clients, func(i, j int) bool {
554: 		if clients[i].proxy.GetClusterID().String() < clients[j].proxy.GetClusterID().String() {
555: 			return true
556: 		}
557: 		if clients[i].proxy.GetNamespace() < clients[j].proxy.GetNamespace() {
558: 			return true
559: 		}
560: 		return clients[i].proxy.GetID() < clients[j].proxy.GetID()
561: 	})
562: 	return clients
563: }
564: 
565: // AllClients returns all connected clients, per Clients, but additionally includes uninitialized connections
566: // Warning: callers must take care not to rely on the con.proxy field being set
567: func (s *DiscoveryServer) AllClients() []*Connection {
568: 	s.adsClientsMutex.RLock()
569: 	defer s.adsClientsMutex.RUnlock()
570: 	return maps.Values(s.adsClients)
571: }

573: func (s *DiscoveryServer) WaitForRequestLimit(ctx context.Context) error {
574: 	if s.RequestRateLimit.Limit() == 0 {
575: 		// Allow opt out when rate limiting is set to 0qps
576: 		return nil
577: 	}
578: 	// Give a bit of time for queue to clear out, but if not fail fast. Client will connect to another
579: 	// instance in best case, or retry with backoff.
580: 	wait, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, time.Second)
581: 	defer cancel()
582: 	return s.RequestRateLimit.Wait(wait)
583: }

262: 	// Apply custom initialization functions.
263: 	for _, fn := range initFuncs {
264: 		fn(s)
265: 	}
266: 	// Initialize workload Trust Bundle before XDS Server
267: 	s.XDSServer = xds.NewDiscoveryServer(e, args.RegistryOptions.KubeOptions.ClusterAliases, args.KrtDebugger)
268: 	configGen := core.NewConfigGenerator(s.XDSServer.Cache)
269: 
270: 	grpcprom.EnableHandlingTimeHistogram()
271: 
272: 	// make sure we have a readiness probe before serving HTTP to avoid marking ready too soon
273: 	s.initReadinessProbes()
274: 
275: 	s.initServers(args)
276: 	if err := s.initIstiodAdminServer(args, s.webhookInfo.GetTemplates); err != nil {
277: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("error initializing debug server: %v", err)
278: 	}
279: 	if err := s.serveHTTP(); err != nil {
280: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("error serving http: %v", err)
281: 	}
282: 
283: 	// Apply the arguments to the configuration.
284: 	if err := s.initKubeClient(args); err != nil {
285: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("error initializing kube client: %v", err)
286: 	}
287: 
288: 	s.initMeshConfiguration(args, s.fileWatcher)
289: 	// Setup Kubernetes watch filters
290: 	// Because this relies on meshconfig, it needs to be outside initKubeClient
291: 	if s.kubeClient != nil {
292: 		// Build a namespace watcher. This must have no filter, since this is our input to the filter itself.
293: 		namespaces := kclient.New[*corev1.Namespace](s.kubeClient)
294: 		filter := namespace.NewDiscoveryNamespacesFilter(namespaces, s.environment.Watcher, s.internalStop)
295: 		s.kubeClient = kubelib.SetObjectFilter(s.kubeClient, filter)
296: 	}
297: 
298: 	s.initMeshNetworks(args, s.fileWatcher)
299: 	s.initMeshHandlers(configGen.MeshConfigChanged)
300: 	s.environment.Init()

46: var (
47: 	log = xds.Log
48: 
49: 	// Tracks connections, increment on each new connection.
50: 	connectionNumber = int64(0)
51: )
52: 
53: // Used only when running in KNative, to handle the load balancing behavior.
54: var firstRequest = uatomic.NewBool(true)
55: 
56: var knativeEnv = env.Register("K_REVISION", "",
57: 	"KNative revision, set if running in knative").Get()
58: 
59: // DiscoveryStream is a server interface for XDS.
60: type DiscoveryStream = xds.DiscoveryStream
61: 
62: // DeltaDiscoveryStream is a server interface for Delta XDS.
63: type DeltaDiscoveryStream = discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_DeltaAggregatedResourcesServer
64: 
65: // DiscoveryClient is a client interface for XDS.
66: type DiscoveryClient = discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_StreamAggregatedResourcesClient
67: 
68: // DeltaDiscoveryClient is a client interface for Delta XDS.
69: type DeltaDiscoveryClient = discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_DeltaAggregatedResourcesClient
70: 
71: type Connection struct {
72: 	xds.Connection
73: 
74: 	// Original node metadata, to avoid unmarshal/marshal.
75: 	// This is included in internal events.
76: 	node *core.Node
77: 
78: 	// proxy is the client to which this connection is established.
79: 	proxy *model.Proxy
80: 
81: 	// deltaStream is used for Delta XDS. Only one of deltaStream or stream will be set
82: 	deltaStream DeltaDiscoveryStream
83: 
84: 	deltaReqChan chan *discovery.DeltaDiscoveryRequest
85: 
86: 	s   *DiscoveryServer
87: 	ids []string
88: }
89: 
90: func (conn *Connection) XdsConnection() *xds.Connection {
91: 	return &conn.Connection
92: }
93: 
94: func (conn *Connection) Proxy() *model.Proxy {
95: 	return conn.proxy
96: }
97: 
98: // Event represents a config or registry event that results in a push.
99: type Event struct {
100: 	// pushRequest PushRequest to use for the push.
101: 	pushRequest *model.PushRequest
102: 
103: 	// function to call once a push is finished. This must be called or future changes may be blocked.
104: 	done func()
105: }
106: 
107: func newConnection(peerAddr string, stream DiscoveryStream) *Connection {
108: 	return &Connection{
109: 		Connection: xds.NewConnection(peerAddr, stream),
110: 	}
111: }
112: 
113: func (conn *Connection) Initialize(node *core.Node) error {
114: 	return conn.s.initConnection(node, conn, conn.ids)
115: }
116: 
117: func (conn *Connection) Close() {
118: 	conn.s.closeConnection(conn)
119: }
120: 
121: func (conn *Connection) Watcher() xds.Watcher {
122: 	return conn.proxy
123: }
124: 
125: func (conn *Connection) Process(req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest) error {
126: 	return conn.s.processRequest(req, conn)
127: }
128: 
129: func (conn *Connection) Push(ev any) error {
130: 	pushEv := ev.(*Event)
131: 	err := conn.s.pushConnection(conn, pushEv)
132: 	pushEv.done()
133: 	return err
134: }
135: 
136: // processRequest handles one discovery request. This is currently called from the 'main' thread, which also
137: // handles 'push' requests and close - the code will eventually call the 'push' code, and it needs more mutex
138: // protection. Original code avoided the mutexes by doing both 'push' and 'process requests' in same thread.
139: func (s *DiscoveryServer) processRequest(req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest, con *Connection) error {
140: 	stype := v3.GetShortType(req.TypeUrl)
141: 	log.Debugf("ADS:%s: REQ %s resources:%d nonce:%s version:%s ", stype,
142: 		con.ID(), len(req.ResourceNames), req.ResponseNonce, req.VersionInfo)
143: 	if req.TypeUrl == v3.HealthInfoType {
144: 		s.handleWorkloadHealthcheck(con.proxy, req)
145: 		return nil
146: 	}
147: 
148: 	// For now, don't let xDS piggyback debug requests start watchers.
149: 	if strings.HasPrefix(req.TypeUrl, v3.DebugType) {
150: 		return s.pushXds(con,
151: 			&model.WatchedResource{TypeUrl: req.TypeUrl, ResourceNames: sets.New(req.ResourceNames...)},
152: 			&model.PushRequest{Full: true, Push: con.proxy.LastPushContext, Forced: true})
153: 	}
154: 
155: 	shouldRespond, delta := xds.ShouldRespond(con.proxy, con.ID(), req)
156: 	if !shouldRespond {
157: 		return nil
158: 	}
159: 
160: 	request := &model.PushRequest{
161: 		Full:   true,
162: 		Push:   con.proxy.LastPushContext,
163: 		Reason: model.NewReasonStats(model.ProxyRequest),
164: 
165: 		// The usage of LastPushTime (rather than time.Now()), is critical here for correctness; This time
166: 		// is used by the XDS cache to determine if a entry is stale. If we use Now() with an old push context,
167: 		// we may end up overriding active cache entries with stale ones.
168: 		Start:  con.proxy.LastPushTime,
169: 		Delta:  delta,
170: 		Forced: true,
171: 	}
172: 
173: 	// SidecarScope for the proxy may not have been updated based on this pushContext.
174: 	// It can happen when `processRequest` comes after push context has been updated(s.initPushContext),
175: 	// but proxy's SidecarScope has been updated(s.computeProxyState -> SetSidecarScope) due to optimizations that skip sidecar scope
176: 	// computation.
177: 	if con.proxy.SidecarScope != nil && con.proxy.SidecarScope.Version != request.Push.PushVersion {
178: 		s.computeProxyState(con.proxy, request)
179: 	}
180: 	return s.pushXds(con, con.proxy.GetWatchedResource(req.TypeUrl), request)
181: }
182: 
183: // StreamAggregatedResources implements the ADS interface.
184: func (s *DiscoveryServer) StreamAggregatedResources(stream DiscoveryStream) error {
185: 	return s.Stream(stream)
186: }
187: 
188: func (s *DiscoveryServer) Stream(stream DiscoveryStream) error {
189: 	if knativeEnv != "" && firstRequest.Load() {
190: 		// How scaling works in knative is the first request is the "loading" request. During
191: 		// loading request, concurrency=1. Once that request is done, concurrency is enabled.
192: 		// However, the XDS stream is long lived, so the first request would block all others. As a
193: 		// result, we should exit the first request immediately; clients will retry.
194: 		firstRequest.Store(false)
195: 		return status.Error(codes.Unavailable, "server warmup not complete; try again")
196: 	}
197: 	// Check if server is ready to accept clients and process new requests.
198: 	// Currently ready means caches have been synced and hence can build
199: 	// clusters correctly. Without this check, InitContext() call below would
200: 	// initialize with empty config, leading to reconnected Envoys losing
201: 	// configuration. This is an additional safety check inaddition to adding
202: 	// cachesSynced logic to readiness probe to handle cases where kube-proxy
203: 	// ip tables update latencies.
204: 	// See https://github.com/istio/istio/issues/25495.
205: 	if !s.IsServerReady() {
206: 		return status.Error(codes.Unavailable, "server is not ready to serve discovery information")
207: 	}
208: 
209: 	ctx := stream.Context()
210: 	peerAddr := "0.0.0.0"
211: 	if peerInfo, ok := peer.FromContext(ctx); ok {
212: 		peerAddr = peerInfo.Addr.String()
213: 	}
214: 
215: 	if err := s.WaitForRequestLimit(stream.Context()); err != nil {
216: 		log.Warnf("ADS: %q exceeded rate limit: %v", peerAddr, err)
217: 		return status.Errorf(codes.ResourceExhausted, "request rate limit exceeded: %v", err)
218: 	}
219: 
220: 	ids, err := s.authenticate(ctx)
221: 	if err != nil {
222: 		return status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, err.Error())
223: 	}
224: 	if ids != nil {
225: 		log.Debugf("Authenticated XDS: %v with identity %v", peerAddr, ids)
226: 	} else {
227: 		log.Debugf("Unauthenticated XDS: %s", peerAddr)
228: 	}
229: 
230: 	// InitContext returns immediately if the context was already initialized.
231: 	s.globalPushContext().InitContext(s.Env, nil, nil)
232: 	con := newConnection(peerAddr, stream)
233: 	con.ids = ids
234: 	con.s = s
235: 	return xds.Stream(con)
236: }
237: 
238: // update the node associated with the connection, after receiving a packet from envoy, also adds the connection
239: // to the tracking map.
240: func (s *DiscoveryServer) initConnection(node *core.Node, con *Connection, identities []string) error {
241: 	// Setup the initial proxy metadata
242: 	proxy, err := s.initProxyMetadata(node)
243: 	if err != nil {
244: 		return err
245: 	}
246: 	// Check if proxy cluster has an alias configured, if yes use that as cluster ID for this proxy.
247: 	if alias, exists := s.ClusterAliases[proxy.Metadata.ClusterID]; exists {
248: 		proxy.Metadata.ClusterID = alias
249: 	}
250: 	// To ensure push context is monotonically increasing, setup LastPushContext before we addCon. This
251: 	// way only new push contexts will be registered for this proxy.
252: 	proxy.LastPushContext = s.globalPushContext()
253: 	// First request so initialize connection id and start tracking it.
254: 	con.SetID(connectionID(proxy.ID))
255: 	con.node = node
256: 	con.proxy = proxy
257: 	if proxy.IsZTunnel() && !features.EnableAmbient {
258: 		return fmt.Errorf("ztunnel requires PILOT_ENABLE_AMBIENT=true")
259: 	}
260: 
261: 	// Authorize xds clients
262: 	if err := s.authorize(con, identities); err != nil {
263: 		return err
264: 	}
265: 
266: 	// Register the connection. this allows pushes to be triggered for the proxy. Note: the timing of
267: 	// this and initializeProxy important. While registering for pushes *after* initialization is complete seems like
268: 	// a better choice, it introduces a race condition; If we complete initialization of a new push
269: 	// context between initializeProxy and addCon, we would not get any pushes triggered for the new
270: 	// push context, leading the proxy to have a stale state until the next full push.
271: 	s.addCon(con.ID(), con)
272: 	// Register that initialization is complete. This triggers to calls that it is safe to access the
273: 	// proxy
274: 	defer con.MarkInitialized()
275: 
276: 	// Complete full initialization of the proxy
277: 	if err := s.initializeProxy(con); err != nil {
278: 		s.closeConnection(con)
279: 		return err
280: 	}
281: 	return nil
282: }
283: 
284: func (s *DiscoveryServer) closeConnection(con *Connection) {
285: 	if con.ID() == "" {
286: 		return
287: 	}
288: 	s.removeCon(con.ID())
289: 	s.WorkloadEntryController.OnDisconnect(con)
290: }
291: 
292: func connectionID(node string) string {
293: 	id := atomic.AddInt64(&connectionNumber, 1)
294: 	return node + "-" + strconv.FormatInt(id, 10)
295: }
296: 
297: // initProxyMetadata initializes just the basic metadata of a proxy. This is decoupled from
298: // initProxyState such that we can perform authorization before attempting expensive computations to
299: // fully initialize the proxy.
300: func (s *DiscoveryServer) initProxyMetadata(node *core.Node) (*model.Proxy, error) {
301: 	meta, err := model.ParseMetadata(node.Metadata)
302: 	if err != nil {
303: 		return nil, status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, err.Error()).Err()
304: 	}
305: 	proxy, err := model.ParseServiceNodeWithMetadata(node.Id, meta)
306: 	if err != nil {
307: 		return nil, status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, err.Error()).Err()
308: 	}
309: 	// Update the config namespace associated with this proxy
310: 	proxy.ConfigNamespace = model.GetProxyConfigNamespace(proxy)
311: 	proxy.XdsNode = node
312: 	return proxy, nil
313: }
314: 
315: // setTopologyLabels sets locality, cluster, network label
316: // must be called after `SetWorkloadLabels` and `SetServiceTargets`.
317: func setTopologyLabels(proxy *model.Proxy) {
318: 	// This is a bit un-intuitive, but pull the locality from Labels first. The service registries have the best access to
319: 	// locality information, as they can read from various sources (Node on Kubernetes, for example). They will take this
320: 	// information and add it to the labels. So while the proxy may not originally have these labels,
321: 	// it will by the time we get here (as a result of calling this after SetWorkloadLabels).
322: 	proxy.Locality = localityFromProxyLabels(proxy)
323: 	if proxy.Locality == nil {
324: 		// If there is no locality in the registry then use the one sent as part of the discovery request.
325: 		// This is not preferable as only the connected Pilot is aware of this proxies location, but it
326: 		// can still help provide some client-side Envoy context when load balancing based on location.
327: 		proxy.Locality = &core.Locality{
328: 			Region:  proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetRegion(),
329: 			Zone:    proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetZone(),
330: 			SubZone: proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetSubZone(),
331: 		}
332: 	}
333: 	// add topology labels to proxy labels
334: 	proxy.Labels = labelutil.AugmentLabels(
335: 		proxy.Labels,
336: 		proxy.Metadata.ClusterID,
337: 		util.LocalityToString(proxy.Locality),
338: 		proxy.GetNodeName(),
339: 		proxy.Metadata.Network,
340: 	)

343: func localityFromProxyLabels(proxy *model.Proxy) *core.Locality {
344: 	region, f1 := proxy.Labels[labelutil.LabelTopologyRegion]
345: 	zone, f2 := proxy.Labels[labelutil.LabelTopologyZone]
346: 	subzone, f3 := proxy.Labels[label.TopologySubzone.Name]
347: 	if !f1 && !f2 && !f3 {
348: 		// If no labels set, we didn't find the locality from the service registry. We do support a (mostly undocumented/internal)
349: 		// label to override the locality, so respect that here as well.
350: 		localityLabel := pm.GetLocalityLabel(proxy.Labels)
351: 		if localityLabel != "" {
352: 			return util.ConvertLocality(localityLabel)
353: 		}
354: 		return nil
355: 	}
356: 	return &core.Locality{
357: 		Region:  region,
358: 		Zone:    zone,
359: 		SubZone: subzone,
360: 	}
361: }

385: func (s *DiscoveryServer) computeProxyState(proxy *model.Proxy, request *model.PushRequest) {
386: 	proxy.Lock()
387: 	defer proxy.Unlock()
388: 	var shouldResetGateway, shouldResetSidecarScope bool
389: 	// 1. If request == nil(initiation phase) or request.ConfigsUpdated == nil(global push), set proxy serviceTargets.
390: 	// 2. otherwise only set when svc update, this is for the case that a service may select the proxy
391: 	if request == nil || request.Forced ||
392: 		proxy.ShouldUpdateServiceTargets(request.ConfigsUpdated) {
393: 		proxy.SetServiceTargets(s.Env.ServiceDiscovery)
394: 		// proxy.SetGatewaysForProxy depends on the serviceTargets,
395: 		// so when we reset serviceTargets, should reset gateway as well.
396: 		shouldResetGateway = true
397: 	}
398: 
399: 	// only recompute workload labels when
400: 	// 1. stream established and proxy first time initialization
401: 	// 2. proxy update
402: 	recomputeLabels := request == nil || request.IsProxyUpdate()
403: 	if recomputeLabels {
404: 		proxy.SetWorkloadLabels(s.Env)
405: 		setTopologyLabels(proxy)
406: 	}
407: 	// Precompute the sidecar scope and merged gateways associated with this proxy.
408: 	// Saves compute cycles in networking code. Though this might be redundant sometimes, we still
409: 	// have to compute this because as part of a config change, a new Sidecar could become
410: 	// applicable to this proxy
411: 	push := proxy.LastPushContext
412: 	if request == nil {
413: 		shouldResetSidecarScope = true
414: 	} else {
415: 		push = request.Push
416: 		if request.Forced {
417: 			shouldResetSidecarScope = true
418: 		}
419: 		for conf := range request.ConfigsUpdated {
420: 			switch conf.Kind {
421: 			case kind.ServiceEntry, kind.DestinationRule, kind.VirtualService, kind.Sidecar:
422: 				shouldResetSidecarScope = true
423: 			case kind.Gateway:
424: 				shouldResetGateway = true
425: 			case kind.Ingress:
426: 				shouldResetSidecarScope = true
427: 				shouldResetGateway = true
428: 			}
429: 			if shouldResetSidecarScope && shouldResetGateway {
430: 				break
431: 			}
432: 		}
433: 	}
434: 	// compute the sidecarscope for both proxy type whenever it changes.
435: 	if shouldResetSidecarScope {
436: 		proxy.SetSidecarScope(push)
437: 	}
438: 	// only compute gateways for "router" type proxy.
439: 	if shouldResetGateway && proxy.Type == model.Router {
440: 		proxy.SetGatewaysForProxy(push)
441: 	}
442: 	proxy.LastPushContext = push
443: 	if request != nil {
444: 		proxy.LastPushTime = request.Start
445: 	}
446: }

471: // Compute and send the new configuration for a connection.
472: func (s *DiscoveryServer) pushConnection(con *Connection, pushEv *Event) error {
473: 	pushRequest := pushEv.pushRequest
474: 
475: 	if pushRequest.Full {
476: 		// Update Proxy with current information.
477: 		s.computeProxyState(con.proxy, pushRequest)
478: 	}
479: 
480: 	pushRequest, needsPush := s.ProxyNeedsPush(con.proxy, pushRequest)
481: 	if !needsPush {
482: 		log.Debugf("Skipping push to %v, no updates required", con.ID())
483: 		return nil
484: 	}
485: 
486: 	// Send pushes to all generators
487: 	// Each Generator is responsible for determining if the push event requires a push
488: 	wrl := con.watchedResourcesByOrder()
489: 	for _, w := range wrl {
490: 		if err := s.pushXds(con, w, pushRequest); err != nil {
491: 			return err
492: 		}
493: 	}
494: 	proxiesConvergeDelay.Record(time.Since(pushRequest.Start).Seconds())
495: 	return nil
496: }

520: func (s *DiscoveryServer) ProxyUpdate(clusterID cluster.ID, ip string) {
521: 	var connection *Connection
522: 
523: 	for _, v := range s.Clients() {
524: 		if v.proxy.Metadata.ClusterID == clusterID && v.proxy.IPAddresses[0] == ip {
525: 			connection = v
526: 			break
527: 		}
528: 	}
529: 
530: 	// It is possible that the envoy has not connected to this pilot, maybe connected to another pilot
531: 	if connection == nil {
532: 		return
533: 	}
534: 	if log.DebugEnabled() {
535: 		currentlyPending := s.pushQueue.Pending()
536: 		if currentlyPending != 0 {
537: 			log.Debugf("Starting new push while %v were still pending", currentlyPending)
538: 		}
539: 	}
540: 
541: 	s.pushQueue.Enqueue(connection, &model.PushRequest{
542: 		Full:   true,
543: 		Push:   s.globalPushContext(),
544: 		Start:  time.Now(),
545: 		Reason: model.NewReasonStats(model.ProxyUpdate),
546: 		Forced: true,
547: 	})
548: }

561: // AdsPushAll will send updates to all nodes, for a full config or incremental EDS.
562: func (s *DiscoveryServer) AdsPushAll(req *model.PushRequest) {
563: 	if !req.Full {
564: 		log.Infof("XDS: Incremental Pushing ConnectedEndpoints:%d Version:%s",
565: 			s.adsClientCount(), req.Push.PushVersion)
566: 	} else {
567: 		totalService := len(req.Push.GetAllServices())
568: 		log.Infof("XDS: Pushing Services:%d ConnectedEndpoints:%d Version:%s",
569: 			totalService, s.adsClientCount(), req.Push.PushVersion)
570: 		monServices.Record(float64(totalService))
571: 
572: 		// Make sure the ConfigsUpdated map exists
573: 		if req.ConfigsUpdated == nil {
574: 			req.ConfigsUpdated = make(sets.Set[model.ConfigKey])
575: 		}
576: 	}
577: 
578: 	s.StartPush(req)
579: }

581: // Send a signal to all connections, with a push event.
582: func (s *DiscoveryServer) StartPush(req *model.PushRequest) {
583: 	// Push config changes, iterating over connected envoys.
584: 	if log.DebugEnabled() {
585: 		currentlyPending := s.pushQueue.Pending()
586: 		if currentlyPending != 0 {
587: 			log.Debugf("Starting new push while %v were still pending", currentlyPending)
588: 		}
589: 	}
590: 	req.Start = time.Now()
591: 	for _, p := range s.AllClients() {
592: 		s.pushQueue.Enqueue(p, req)
593: 	}
594: }

620: // watchedResourcesByOrder returns the ordered list of
621: // watched resources for the proxy, ordered in accordance with known push order.
622: func (conn *Connection) watchedResourcesByOrder() []*model.WatchedResource {
623: 	allWatched := conn.proxy.ShallowCloneWatchedResources()
624: 	ordered := make([]*model.WatchedResource, 0, len(allWatched))
625: 	// first add all known types, in order
626: 	for _, tp := range PushOrder {
627: 		if allWatched[tp] != nil {
628: 			ordered = append(ordered, allWatched[tp])
629: 		}
630: 	}
631: 	// Then add any undeclared types
632: 	for tp, res := range allWatched {
633: 		if !KnownOrderedTypeUrls.Contains(tp) {
634: 			ordered = append(ordered, res)
635: 		}
636: 	}
637: 	return ordered
638: }

43: func (s *DiscoveryServer) StreamDeltas(stream DeltaDiscoveryStream) error {
44: 	if knativeEnv != "" && firstRequest.Load() {
45: 		// How scaling works in knative is the first request is the "loading" request. During
46: 		// loading request, concurrency=1. Once that request is done, concurrency is enabled.
47: 		// However, the XDS stream is long lived, so the first request would block all others. As a
48: 		// result, we should exit the first request immediately; clients will retry.
49: 		firstRequest.Store(false)
50: 		return status.Error(codes.Unavailable, "server warmup not complete; try again")
51: 	}
52: 	// Check if server is ready to accept clients and process new requests.
53: 	// Currently ready means caches have been synced and hence can build
54: 	// clusters correctly. Without this check, InitContext() call below would
55: 	// initialize with empty config, leading to reconnected Envoys losing
56: 	// configuration. This is an additional safety check inaddition to adding
57: 	// cachesSynced logic to readiness probe to handle cases where kube-proxy
58: 	// ip tables update latencies.
59: 	// See https://github.com/istio/istio/issues/25495.
60: 	if !s.IsServerReady() {
61: 		return errors.New("server is not ready to serve discovery information")
62: 	}
63: 
64: 	ctx := stream.Context()
65: 	peerAddr := "0.0.0.0"
66: 	if peerInfo, ok := peer.FromContext(ctx); ok {
67: 		peerAddr = peerInfo.Addr.String()
68: 	}
69: 
70: 	if err := s.WaitForRequestLimit(stream.Context()); err != nil {
71: 		deltaLog.Warnf("ADS: %q exceeded rate limit: %v", peerAddr, err)
72: 		return status.Errorf(codes.ResourceExhausted, "request rate limit exceeded: %v", err)
73: 	}
74: 
75: 	ids, err := s.authenticate(ctx)
76: 	if err != nil {
77: 		return status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, err.Error())
78: 	}
79: 	if ids != nil {
80: 		deltaLog.Debugf("Authenticated XDS: %v with identity %v", peerAddr, ids)
81: 	} else {
82: 		deltaLog.Debugf("Unauthenticated XDS: %v", peerAddr)
83: 	}
84: 
85: 	// InitContext returns immediately if the context was already initialized.
86: 	s.globalPushContext().InitContext(s.Env, nil, nil)
87: 	con := newDeltaConnection(peerAddr, stream)
88: 
89: 	// Do not call: defer close(con.pushChannel). The push channel will be garbage collected
90: 	// when the connection is no longer used. Closing the channel can cause subtle race conditions
91: 	// with push. According to the spec: "It's only necessary to close a channel when it is important
92: 	// to tell the receiving goroutines that all data have been sent."
93: 
94: 	// Block until either a request is received or a push is triggered.
95: 	// We need 2 go routines because 'read' blocks in Recv().
96: 	go s.receiveDelta(con, ids)
97: 
98: 	// Wait for the proxy to be fully initialized before we start serving traffic. Because
99: 	// initialization doesn't have dependencies that will block, there is no need to add any timeout
100: 	// here. Prior to this explicit wait, we were implicitly waiting by receive() not sending to

70: 	if err := s.WaitForRequestLimit(stream.Context()); err != nil {
71: 		deltaLog.Warnf("ADS: %q exceeded rate limit: %v", peerAddr, err)
72: 		return status.Errorf(codes.ResourceExhausted, "request rate limit exceeded: %v", err)
73: 	}
74: 
75: 	ids, err := s.authenticate(ctx)
76: 	if err != nil {
77: 		return status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, err.Error())
78: 	}
79: 	if ids != nil {
80: 		deltaLog.Debugf("Authenticated XDS: %v with identity %v", peerAddr, ids)
81: 	} else {
82: 		deltaLog.Debugf("Unauthenticated XDS: %v", peerAddr)
83: 	}
84: 
85: 	// InitContext returns immediately if the context was already initialized.
86: 	s.globalPushContext().InitContext(s.Env, nil, nil)
87: 	con := newDeltaConnection(peerAddr, stream)
88: 
89: 	// Do not call: defer close(con.pushChannel). The push channel will be garbage collected
90: 	// when the connection is no longer used. Closing the channel can cause subtle race conditions
91: 	// with push. According to the spec: "It's only necessary to close a channel when it is important
92: 	// to tell the receiving goroutines that all data have been sent."
93: 
94: 	// Block until either a request is received or a push is triggered.
95: 	// We need 2 go routines because 'read' blocks in Recv().
96: 	go s.receiveDelta(con, ids)
97: 
98: 	// Wait for the proxy to be fully initialized before we start serving traffic. Because
99: 	// initialization doesn't have dependencies that will block, there is no need to add any timeout
100: 	// here. Prior to this explicit wait, we were implicitly waiting by receive() not sending to
101: 	// reqChannel and the connection not being enqueued for pushes to pushChannel until the
102: 	// initialization is complete.
103: 	<-con.InitializedCh()
104: 
105: 	for {
106: 		// Go select{} statements are not ordered; the same channel can be chosen many times.
107: 		// For requests, these are higher priority (client may be blocked on startup until these are done)
108: 		// and often very cheap to handle (simple ACK), so we check it first.
109: 		select {
110: 		case req, ok := <-con.deltaReqChan:
111: 			if ok {
112: 				if err := s.processDeltaRequest(req, con); err != nil {
113: 					return err
114: 				}
115: 			} else {
116: 				// Remote side closed connection or error processing the request.
117: 				return <-con.ErrorCh()
118: 			}
119: 		case <-con.StopCh():
120: 			return nil
121: 		default:
122: 		}
123: 		// If there wasn't already a request, poll for requests and pushes. Note: if we have a huge
124: 		// amount of incoming requests, we may still send some pushes, as we do not `continue` above;
125: 		// however, requests will be handled ~2x as much as pushes. This ensures a wave of requests
126: 		// cannot completely starve pushes. However, this scenario is unlikely.
127: 		select {
128: 		case req, ok := <-con.deltaReqChan:
129: 			if ok {
130: 				if err := s.processDeltaRequest(req, con); err != nil {
131: 					return err
132: 				}
133: 			} else {
134: 				// Remote side closed connection or error processing the request.
135: 				return <-con.ErrorCh()
136: 			}
137: 		case ev := <-con.PushCh():
138: 			pushEv := ev.(*Event)
139: 			err := s.pushConnectionDelta(con, pushEv)
140: 			pushEv.done()
141: 			if err != nil {
142: 				return err
143: 			}
144: 		case <-con.StopCh():
145: 			return nil
146: 		}
147: 	}
148: }
149: 
150: // Compute and send the new configuration for a connection.
151: func (s *DiscoveryServer) pushConnectionDelta(con *Connection, pushEv *Event) error {
152: 	pushRequest := pushEv.pushRequest
153: 
154: 	if pushRequest.Full {
155: 		// Update Proxy with current information.
156: 		s.computeProxyState(con.proxy, pushRequest)
157: 	}
158: 
159: 	pushRequest, needsPush := s.ProxyNeedsPush(con.proxy, pushRequest)
160: 	if !needsPush {
161: 		deltaLog.Debugf("Skipping push to %v, no updates required", con.ID())
162: 		return nil
163: 	}
164: 
165: 	// Send pushes to all generators
166: 	// Each Generator is responsible for determining if the push event requires a push
167: 	wrl := con.watchedResourcesByOrder()
168: 	for _, w := range wrl {
169: 		if err := s.pushDeltaXds(con, w, pushRequest); err != nil {
170: 			return err
171: 		}
172: 	}
173: 
174: 	proxiesConvergeDelay.Record(time.Since(pushRequest.Start).Seconds())
175: 	return nil
176: }

178: func (s *DiscoveryServer) receiveDelta(con *Connection, identities []string) {
179: 	defer func() {
180: 		close(con.deltaReqChan)
181: 		close(con.ErrorCh())
182: 		// Close the initialized channel, if its not already closed, to prevent blocking the stream
183: 		select {
184: 		case <-con.InitializedCh():
185: 		default:
186: 			close(con.InitializedCh())
187: 		}
188: 	}()
189: 	firstRequest := true
190: 	for {
191: 		req, err := con.deltaStream.Recv()
192: 		if err != nil {
193: 			if istiogrpc.GRPCErrorType(err) != istiogrpc.UnexpectedError {
194: 				deltaLog.Infof("ADS: %q %s terminated", con.Peer(), con.ID())
195: 				return
196: 			}
197: 			con.ErrorCh() <- err
198: 			deltaLog.Errorf("ADS: %q %s terminated with error: %v", con.Peer(), con.ID(), err)
199: 			xds.TotalXDSInternalErrors.Increment()
200: 			return
201: 		}
202: 		// This should be only set for the first request. The node id may not be set - for example malicious clients.
203: 		if firstRequest {
204: 			// probe happens before envoy sends first xDS request
205: 			if req.TypeUrl == v3.HealthInfoType {
206: 				log.Warnf("ADS: %q %s send health check probe before normal xDS request", con.Peer(), con.ID())
207: 				continue
208: 			}
209: 			firstRequest = false
210: 			if req.Node == nil || req.Node.Id == "" {
211: 				con.ErrorCh() <- status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, "missing node information").Err()
212: 				return
213: 			}
214: 			if err := s.initConnection(req.Node, con, identities); err != nil {
215: 				con.ErrorCh() <- err
216: 				return
217: 			}
218: 			defer s.closeConnection(con)
219: 			deltaLog.Infof("ADS: new delta connection for node:%s", con.ID())
220: 		}
221: 
222: 		select {
223: 		case con.deltaReqChan <- req:
224: 		case <-con.deltaStream.Context().Done():
225: 			deltaLog.Infof("ADS: %q %s terminated with stream closed", con.Peer(), con.ID())
226: 			return
227: 		}
228: 	}
229: }

231: func (conn *Connection) sendDelta(res *discovery.DeltaDiscoveryResponse, newResourceNames sets.String) error {
232: 	sendResonse := func() error {
233: 		start := time.Now()
234: 		defer func() { xds.RecordSendTime(time.Since(start)) }()
235: 		return conn.deltaStream.Send(res)
236: 	}
237: 	err := sendResonse()
238: 	if err == nil {
239: 		if !strings.HasPrefix(res.TypeUrl, v3.DebugType) {
240: 			conn.proxy.UpdateWatchedResource(res.TypeUrl, func(wr *model.WatchedResource) *model.WatchedResource {
241: 				if wr == nil {
242: 					wr = &model.WatchedResource{TypeUrl: res.TypeUrl}
243: 				}
244: 				// some resources dynamically update ResourceNames. Most don't though
245: 				if newResourceNames != nil {
246: 					wr.ResourceNames = newResourceNames
247: 				}
248: 				wr.NonceSent = res.Nonce
249: 				wr.LastSendTime = time.Now()
250: 				if features.EnableUnsafeDeltaTest {
251: 					wr.LastResources = applyDelta(wr.LastResources, res)
252: 				}
253: 				return wr
254: 			})
255: 		}
256: 	} else if status.Convert(err).Code() == codes.DeadlineExceeded {
257: 		deltaLog.Infof("Timeout writing %s: %v", conn.ID(), v3.GetShortType(res.TypeUrl))
258: 		xds.ResponseWriteTimeouts.Increment()
259: 	}
260: 	return err
261: }
262: 
263: // processDeltaRequest is handling one request. This is currently called from the 'main' thread, which also
264: // handles 'push' requests and close - the code will eventually call the 'push' code, and it needs more mutex
265: // protection. Original code avoided the mutexes by doing both 'push' and 'process requests' in same thread.
266: func (s *DiscoveryServer) processDeltaRequest(req *discovery.DeltaDiscoveryRequest, con *Connection) error {
267: 	stype := v3.GetShortType(req.TypeUrl)
268: 	deltaLog.Debugf("ADS:%s: REQ %s resources sub:%d unsub:%d nonce:%s", stype,
269: 		con.ID(), len(req.ResourceNamesSubscribe), len(req.ResourceNamesUnsubscribe), req.ResponseNonce)
270: 
271: 	if req.TypeUrl == v3.HealthInfoType {
272: 		s.handleWorkloadHealthcheck(con.proxy, deltaToSotwRequest(req))
273: 		return nil
274: 	}
275: 	if strings.HasPrefix(req.TypeUrl, v3.DebugType) {
276: 		return s.pushDeltaXds(con,
277: 			&model.WatchedResource{TypeUrl: req.TypeUrl, ResourceNames: sets.New(req.ResourceNamesSubscribe...)},
278: 			&model.PushRequest{Full: true, Push: con.proxy.LastPushContext, Forced: true})
279: 	}
280: 
281: 	shouldRespond := shouldRespondDelta(con, req)
282: 	if !shouldRespond {
283: 		return nil
284: 	}
285: 
286: 	subs, _, _ := deltaWatchedResources(nil, req)
287: 	request := &model.PushRequest{
288: 		Full:   true,
289: 		Push:   con.proxy.LastPushContext,
290: 		Reason: model.NewReasonStats(model.ProxyRequest),
291: 
292: 		// The usage of LastPushTime (rather than time.Now()), is critical here for correctness; This time
293: 		// is used by the XDS cache to determine if a entry is stale. If we use Now() with an old push context,
294: 		// we may end up overriding active cache entries with stale ones.
295: 		Start: con.proxy.LastPushTime,
296: 		Delta: model.ResourceDelta{
297: 			// Record sub/unsub, but drop synthetic wildcard info
298: 			Subscribed:   subs,
299: 			Unsubscribed: sets.New(req.ResourceNamesUnsubscribe...).Delete("*"),
300: 		},
301: 		Forced: true,
302: 	}
303: 	// SidecarScope for the proxy may has not been updated based on this pushContext.
304: 	// It can happen when `processRequest` comes after push context has been updated(s.initPushContext),
305: 	// but before proxy's SidecarScope has been updated(s.updateProxy).
306: 	if con.proxy.SidecarScope != nil && con.proxy.SidecarScope.Version != request.Push.PushVersion {
307: 		s.computeProxyState(con.proxy, request)
308: 	}
309: 
310: 	err := s.pushDeltaXds(con, con.proxy.GetWatchedResource(req.TypeUrl), request)
311: 	if err != nil {
312: 		return err
313: 	}
314: 	// Anytime we get a CDS request on reconnect, we should always push EDS as well.
315: 	// It is always the server's responsibility to send EDS after CDS, regardless if
316: 	// Envoy asks for it or not (See https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/issues/33607 for more details).
317: 	// Without this logic, there are cases where the clusters we send could stay warming forever,
318: 	// expecting an EDS response. Note that in SotW, Envoy sends an EDS request after the delayed
319: 	// CDS request; however, this is not guaranteed in delta, and has been observed to cause issues
320: 	// with EDS and SDS.
321: 	// This can happen with the following sequence
322: 	// 1. Envoy disconnects and reconnects to Istiod.
323: 	// 2. Envoy sends EDS request and we respond with it.
324: 	// 3. Envoy sends CDS request and we respond with clusters.
325: 	// 4. Envoy detects a change in cluster state and tries to warm those clusters but never sends
326: 	//    an EDS request for them.
327: 	// 5. Therefore, any initial CDS request should always trigger an EDS response
328: 	// 	  to let Envoy finish cluster warming.
329: 	// Refer to https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/issues/13009 for some more details on this type of issues.
330: 	if req.TypeUrl != v3.ClusterType {
331: 		return nil
332: 	}
333: 	return s.forceEDSPush(con)
334: }
335: 
336: func (s *DiscoveryServer) forceEDSPush(con *Connection) error {
337: 	if dwr := con.proxy.GetWatchedResource(v3.EndpointType); dwr != nil {
338: 		request := &model.PushRequest{
339: 			Full:   true,
340: 			Push:   con.proxy.LastPushContext,
341: 			Reason: model.NewReasonStats(model.DependentResource),
342: 			Start:  con.proxy.LastPushTime,
343: 			Forced: true,
344: 		}
345: 		deltaLog.Infof("ADS:%s: FORCE %s PUSH for warming.", v3.GetShortType(v3.EndpointType), con.ID())
346: 		return s.pushDeltaXds(con, dwr, request)
347: 	}
348: 	return nil
349: }

351: // shouldRespondDelta determines whether this request needs to be responded back. It applies the ack/nack rules as per xds protocol
352: // using WatchedResource for previous state and discovery request for the current state.
353: func shouldRespondDelta(con *Connection, request *discovery.DeltaDiscoveryRequest) bool {
354: 	stype := v3.GetShortType(request.TypeUrl)
355: 
356: 	// If there is an error in request that means previous response is erroneous.
357: 	// We do not have to respond in that case. In this case request's version info
358: 	// will be different from the version sent. But it is fragile to rely on that.
359: 	if request.ErrorDetail != nil {
360: 		errCode := codes.Code(request.ErrorDetail.Code)
361: 		deltaLog.Warnf("ADS:%s: ACK ERROR %s %s:%s", stype, con.ID(), errCode.String(), request.ErrorDetail.GetMessage())
362: 		xds.IncrementXDSRejects(request.TypeUrl, con.proxy.ID, errCode.String())
363: 		con.proxy.UpdateWatchedResource(request.TypeUrl, func(wr *model.WatchedResource) *model.WatchedResource {
364: 			wr.LastError = request.ErrorDetail.GetMessage()
365: 			return wr
366: 		})
367: 		return false
368: 	}
369: 
370: 	deltaLog.Debugf("ADS:%s REQUEST %v: sub:%v unsub:%v initial:%v", stype, con.ID(),
371: 		request.ResourceNamesSubscribe, request.ResourceNamesUnsubscribe, request.InitialResourceVersions)
372: 	previousInfo := con.proxy.GetWatchedResource(request.TypeUrl)
373: 
374: 	// This can happen in two cases:
375: 	// 1. Envoy initially send request to Istiod
376: 	// 2. Envoy reconnect to Istiod i.e. Istiod does not have
377: 	// information about this typeUrl, but Envoy sends response nonce - either
378: 	// because Istiod is restarted or Envoy disconnects and reconnects.
379: 	// We should always respond with the current resource names.
380: 	if previousInfo == nil {
381: 		con.proxy.Lock()
382: 		defer con.proxy.Unlock()
383: 
384: 		if len(request.InitialResourceVersions) > 0 {
385: 			deltaLog.Debugf("ADS:%s: RECONNECT %s %s resources:%v", stype, con.ID(), request.ResponseNonce, len(request.InitialResourceVersions))
386: 		} else {
387: 			deltaLog.Debugf("ADS:%s: INIT %s %s", stype, con.ID(), request.ResponseNonce)
388: 		}
389: 
390: 		res, wildcard, _ := deltaWatchedResources(nil, request)
391: 		skip := request.TypeUrl == v3.AddressType && wildcard
392: 		if skip {
393: 			// Due to the high resource count in WDS at scale, we do not store ResourceName.
394: 			// See the workload generator for more information on why we don't use this.
395: 			res = nil
396: 		}
397: 		con.proxy.WatchedResources[request.TypeUrl] = &model.WatchedResource{
398: 			TypeUrl:       request.TypeUrl,
399: 			ResourceNames: res,
400: 			Wildcard:      wildcard,
401: 		}
402: 		return true
403: 	}
404: 
405: 	// If there is mismatch in the nonce, that is a case of expired/stale nonce.
406: 	// A nonce becomes stale following a newer nonce being sent to Envoy.
407: 	if request.ResponseNonce != "" && request.ResponseNonce != previousInfo.NonceSent {
408: 		deltaLog.Debugf("ADS:%s: REQ %s Expired nonce received %s, sent %s", stype,
409: 			con.ID(), request.ResponseNonce, previousInfo.NonceSent)
410: 		xds.ExpiredNonce.With(typeTag.Value(v3.GetMetricType(request.TypeUrl))).Increment()
411: 		return false
412: 	}
413: 
414: 	// Spontaneous DeltaDiscoveryRequests from the client.
415: 	// This can be done to dynamically add or remove elements from the tracked resource_names set.
416: 	// In this case response_nonce is empty.
417: 	spontaneousReq := request.ResponseNonce == ""
418: 
419: 	var alwaysRespond bool
420: 	var subChanged bool
421: 
422: 	// Update resource names, and record ACK if required.
423: 	con.proxy.UpdateWatchedResource(request.TypeUrl, func(wr *model.WatchedResource) *model.WatchedResource {
424: 		wr.ResourceNames, _, subChanged = deltaWatchedResources(wr.ResourceNames, request)
425: 		if !spontaneousReq {
426: 			// Clear last error, we got an ACK.
427: 			// Otherwise, this is just a change in resource subscription, so leave the last ACK info in place.
428: 			wr.LastError = ""
429: 			wr.NonceAcked = request.ResponseNonce
430: 		}
431: 		alwaysRespond = wr.AlwaysRespond
432: 		wr.AlwaysRespond = false
433: 		return wr
434: 	})
435: 
436: 	// It is invalid in the below two cases:
437: 	// 1. no subscribed resources change from spontaneous delta request.
438: 	// 2. subscribed resources changes from ACK.
439: 	if spontaneousReq && !subChanged || !spontaneousReq && subChanged {
440: 		deltaLog.Errorf("ADS:%s: Subscribed resources check mismatch: %v vs %v", stype, spontaneousReq, subChanged)
441: 		if features.EnableUnsafeAssertions {
442: 			panic(fmt.Sprintf("ADS:%s: Subscribed resources check mismatch: %v vs %v", stype, spontaneousReq, subChanged))
443: 		}
444: 	}
445: 
446: 	// Envoy can send two DiscoveryRequests with same version and nonce
447: 	// when it detects a new resource. We should respond if they change.
448: 	if !subChanged {
449: 		// We should always respond "alwaysRespond" marked requests to let Envoy finish warming
450: 		// even though Nonce match and it looks like an ACK.
451: 		if alwaysRespond {
452: 			deltaLog.Infof("ADS:%s: FORCE RESPONSE %s for warming.", stype, con.ID())
453: 			return true
454: 		}
455: 
456: 		deltaLog.Debugf("ADS:%s: ACK %s %s", stype, con.ID(), request.ResponseNonce)
457: 		return false
458: 	}
459: 	deltaLog.Debugf("ADS:%s: RESOURCE CHANGE %s %s", stype, con.ID(), request.ResponseNonce)
460: 
461: 	return true
462: }

464: // Push a Delta XDS resource for the given connection.
465: func (s *DiscoveryServer) pushDeltaXds(con *Connection, w *model.WatchedResource, req *model.PushRequest) error {
466: 	if w == nil {
467: 		return nil
468: 	}
469: 	gen := s.findGenerator(w.TypeUrl, con)
470: 	if gen == nil {
471: 		return nil
472: 	}
473: 	t0 := time.Now()
474: 
475: 	originalW := w
476: 	// If delta is set, client is requesting new resources or removing old ones. We should just generate the
477: 	// new resources it needs, rather than the entire set of known resources.
478: 	// Note: we do not need to account for unsubscribed resources as these are handled by parent removal;
479: 	// See https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/api-docs/xds_protocol#deleting-resources.
480: 	// This means if there are only removals, we will not respond.
481: 	var logFiltered string
482: 	if !req.Delta.IsEmpty() && !requiresResourceNamesModification(w.TypeUrl) {
483: 		// Some types opt out of this and natively handle req.Delta
484: 		logFiltered = " filtered:" + strconv.Itoa(len(w.ResourceNames)-len(req.Delta.Subscribed))
485: 		w = &model.WatchedResource{
486: 			TypeUrl:       w.TypeUrl,
487: 			ResourceNames: req.Delta.Subscribed,
488: 		}
489: 	}
490: 
491: 	var res model.Resources
492: 	var deletedRes model.DeletedResources
493: 	var logdata model.XdsLogDetails
494: 	var usedDelta bool
495: 	var err error
496: 	switch g := gen.(type) {
497: 	case model.XdsDeltaResourceGenerator:
498: 		res, deletedRes, logdata, usedDelta, err = g.GenerateDeltas(con.proxy, req, w)
499: 		if features.EnableUnsafeDeltaTest {
500: 			fullRes, l, _ := g.Generate(con.proxy, originalW, req)
501: 			s.compareDiff(con, originalW, fullRes, res, deletedRes, usedDelta, req.Delta, l.Incremental)
502: 		}
503: 	case model.XdsResourceGenerator:
504: 		res, logdata, err = g.Generate(con.proxy, w, req)
505: 	}
506: 	if err != nil || (res == nil && deletedRes == nil) {
507: 		return err
508: 	}
509: 	defer func() { recordPushTime(w.TypeUrl, time.Since(t0)) }()
510: 	resp := &discovery.DeltaDiscoveryResponse{
511: 		ControlPlane: ControlPlane(w.TypeUrl),
512: 		TypeUrl:      w.TypeUrl,
513: 		// TODO: send different version for incremental eds
514: 		SystemVersionInfo: req.Push.PushVersion,
515: 		Nonce:             nonce(req.Push.PushVersion),
516: 		Resources:         res,
517: 	}
518: 	if usedDelta {
519: 		resp.RemovedResources = deletedRes
520: 	} else if req.Full {
521: 		// similar to sotw
522: 		removed := w.ResourceNames.Copy()
523: 		for _, r := range res {
524: 			removed.Delete(r.Name)
525: 		}
526: 		resp.RemovedResources = sets.SortedList(removed)
527: 	}
528: 	var newResourceNames sets.String
529: 	if shouldSetWatchedResources(w) {
530: 		// Set the new watched resources. Do not write to w directly, as it can be a copy from the 'filtered' logic above
531: 		if usedDelta {
532: 			// Apply the delta
533: 			newResourceNames = w.ResourceNames.Copy().
534: 				DeleteAll(resp.RemovedResources...)
535: 			for _, r := range res {
536: 				newResourceNames.Insert(r.Name)
537: 			}
538: 		} else {
539: 			newResourceNames = resourceNamesSet(res)
540: 		}
541: 	}
542: 	if neverRemoveDelta(w.TypeUrl) {
543: 		resp.RemovedResources = nil
544: 	}
545: 	if len(resp.RemovedResources) > 0 {
546: 		deltaLog.Debugf("ADS:%v REMOVE for node:%s %v", v3.GetShortType(w.TypeUrl), con.ID(), resp.RemovedResources)
547: 	}
548: 
549: 	configSize := ResourceSize(res)
550: 	configSizeBytes.With(typeTag.Value(w.TypeUrl)).Record(float64(configSize))
551: 
552: 	ptype := "PUSH"
553: 	info := ""
554: 	if logdata.Incremental {
555: 		ptype = "PUSH INC"
556: 	}
557: 	if len(logdata.AdditionalInfo) > 0 {
558: 		info = " " + logdata.AdditionalInfo
559: 	}
560: 	if len(logFiltered) > 0 {
561: 		info += logFiltered
562: 	}
563: 
564: 	if err := con.sendDelta(resp, newResourceNames); err != nil {
565: 		logger := deltaLog.Debugf
566: 		if recordSendError(w.TypeUrl, err) {
567: 			logger = deltaLog.Warnf
568: 		}
569: 		if deltaLog.DebugEnabled() {
570: 			logger("%s: Send failure for node:%s resources:%d size:%s%s: %v",
571: 				v3.GetShortType(w.TypeUrl), con.proxy.ID, len(res), util.ByteCount(configSize), info, err)
572: 		}
573: 		return err
574: 	}
575: 
576: 	switch {
577: 	case !req.Full:
578: 		if deltaLog.DebugEnabled() {
579: 			deltaLog.Debugf("%s: %s%s for node:%s resources:%d size:%s%s",
580: 				v3.GetShortType(w.TypeUrl), ptype, req.PushReason(), con.proxy.ID, len(res), util.ByteCount(configSize), info)
581: 		}
582: 	default:
583: 		debug := ""
584: 		if deltaLog.DebugEnabled() {
585: 			// Add additional information to logs when debug mode enabled.
586: 			debug = " nonce:" + resp.Nonce + " version:" + resp.SystemVersionInfo
587: 		}
588: 		deltaLog.Infof("%s: %s%s for node:%s resources:%d removed:%d size:%v%s%s",
589: 			v3.GetShortType(w.TypeUrl), ptype, req.PushReason(), con.proxy.ID, len(res), len(resp.RemovedResources),
590: 			util.ByteCount(ResourceSize(res)), info, debug)
591: 	}
592: 
593: 	return nil
594: }

596: func resourceNamesSet(res model.Resources) sets.Set[string] {
597: 	return sets.New(slices.Map(res, func(r *discovery.Resource) string {
598: 		return r.Name
599: 	})...)
600: }
601: 
602: // requiresResourceNamesModification checks if a generator needs mutable access to w.ResourceNames.
603: // This is used when resources are spontaneously pushed during Delta XDS
604: func requiresResourceNamesModification(url string) bool {
605: 	return url == v3.AddressType || url == v3.WorkloadType
606: }
607: 
608: // neverRemoveDelta checks if a type should never remove resources
609: func neverRemoveDelta(url string) bool {
610: 	// https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/issues/32823
611: 	// We want to garbage collect extensions when they are no longer referenced, rather than delete immediately
612: 	return url == v3.ExtensionConfigurationType
613: }
614: 
615: // shouldSetWatchedResources indicates whether we should set the watched resources for a given type.
616: // for some type like `Address` we customly handle it in the generator
617: func shouldSetWatchedResources(w *model.WatchedResource) bool {
618: 	if requiresResourceNamesModification(w.TypeUrl) {
619: 		// These handle it directly in the generator
620: 		return false
621: 	}
622: 	// Else fallback based on type
623: 	return xds.IsWildcardTypeURL(w.TypeUrl)
624: }
625: 
626: func newDeltaConnection(peerAddr string, stream DeltaDiscoveryStream) *Connection {
627: 	return &Connection{
628: 		Connection:   xds.NewConnection(peerAddr, nil),
629: 		deltaStream:  stream,
630: 		deltaReqChan: make(chan *discovery.DeltaDiscoveryRequest, 1),
631: 	}
632: }
633: 
634: // To satisfy methods that need DiscoveryRequest. Not suitable for real usage
635: func deltaToSotwRequest(request *discovery.DeltaDiscoveryRequest) *discovery.DiscoveryRequest {
636: 	return &discovery.DiscoveryRequest{
637: 		Node:          request.Node,
638: 		ResourceNames: request.ResourceNamesSubscribe,
639: 		TypeUrl:       request.TypeUrl,
640: 		ResponseNonce: request.ResponseNonce,
641: 		ErrorDetail:   request.ErrorDetail,
642: 	}
643: }
644: 
645: // deltaWatchedResources returns current watched resources of delta xds
646: func deltaWatchedResources(existing sets.String, request *discovery.DeltaDiscoveryRequest) (sets.String, bool, bool) {
647: 	res := existing
648: 	if res == nil {
649: 		res = sets.New[string]()
650: 	}
651: 	changed := false
652: 	for _, r := range request.ResourceNamesSubscribe {
653: 		if !res.InsertContains(r) {
654: 			changed = true
655: 		}
656: 	}
657: 	// This is set by Envoy on first request on reconnection so that we are aware of what Envoy knows
658: 	// and can continue the xDS session properly.
659: 	for r := range request.InitialResourceVersions {
660: 		if !res.InsertContains(r) {
661: 			changed = true
662: 		}
663: 	}
664: 	for _, r := range request.ResourceNamesUnsubscribe {
665: 		if res.DeleteContains(r) {
666: 			changed = true
667: 		}
668: 	}
669: 	wildcard := false
670: 	// A request is wildcard if they explicitly subscribe to "*" or subscribe to nothing
671: 	if res.Contains("*") {
672: 		wildcard = true
673: 		res.Delete("*")
674: 	}
675: 	// "if the client sends a request but has never explicitly subscribed to any resource names, the
676: 	// server should treat that identically to how it would treat the client having explicitly
677: 	// subscribed to *"
678: 	// NOTE: this means you cannot subscribe to nothing, which is useful for on-demand loading; to workaround this
679: 	// Istio clients will send and initial request both subscribing+unsubscribing to `*`.
680: 	if len(request.ResourceNamesSubscribe) == 0 {
681: 		wildcard = true
682: 	}
683: 	return res, wildcard, changed
684: }

28: 	Server *DiscoveryServer

31: var (
32: 	_ model.XdsResourceGenerator      = &WorkloadGenerator{}
33: 	_ model.XdsDeltaResourceGenerator = &WorkloadGenerator{}
34: )
35: 
36: // GenerateDeltas computes Workload resources. This is design to be highly optimized to delta updates,
37: // and supports *on-demand* client usage. A client can subscribe with a wildcard subscription and get all
38: // resources (with delta updates), or on-demand and only get responses for specifically subscribed resources.
39: //
40: // Incoming requests may be for VIP or Pod IP addresses. However, all responses are Workload resources, which are pod based.
41: // This means subscribing to a VIP may end up pushing many resources of different name than the request.
42: // On-demand clients are expected to handle this (for wildcard, this is not applicable, as they don't specify any resources at all).
43: func (e WorkloadGenerator) GenerateDeltas(
44: 	proxy *model.Proxy,
45: 	req *model.PushRequest,
46: 	w *model.WatchedResource,
47: ) (model.Resources, model.DeletedResources, model.XdsLogDetails, bool, error) {
48: 	addresses := req.AddressesUpdated
49: 	isReq := req.IsRequest()
50: 	if !isReq && len(addresses) == 0 {
51: 		// Nothing changed...
52: 		return nil, nil, model.XdsLogDetails{}, false, nil
53: 	}
54: 
55: 	if !w.Wildcard {
56: 		return e.generateDeltasOndemand(proxy, req, w)
57: 	}
58: 
59: 	reqAddresses := addresses
60: 	if isReq {
61: 		reqAddresses = nil
62: 	}
63: 	addrs, removed := e.Server.Env.ServiceDiscovery.AddressInformation(reqAddresses)
64: 	// Note: while "removed" is a weird name for a resource that never existed, this is how the spec works:
65: 	// https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/api-docs/xds_protocol#id2
66: 	have := sets.New[string]()
67: 	resources := make(model.Resources, 0, len(addrs))
68: 	for _, addr := range addrs {
69: 		resources = appendAddress(addr, w.TypeUrl, nil, have, resources)
70: 	}
71: 
72: 	if isReq {
73: 		// If it's a full push, AddressInformation won't have info to compute the full set of removals.
74: 		// Instead, we need can see what resources are missing that we were subscribe to; those were removed.
75: 		// During a request, a client will send a subscription request with its current state, so our removals will be:
76: 		// ZtunnelCurrentResources - IstiodCurrentResources (+ empty `removed`).
77: 		removed = req.Delta.Subscribed.Difference(have).Merge(removed)
78: 	}
79: 
80: 	// Due to the high resource count in WDS at scale, we elide updating ResourceNames here.
81: 	// A name will be ~50-100 bytes. So 50k pods would be 5MB per XDS client (plus overheads around GC, sets, fragmentation, etc).
82: 	// Fortunately, we do not actually need this: the only time we need to know the state in Ztunnel is on reconnection which we handle from
83: 	// `req.Delta.Subscribed`.
84: 
85: 	return resources, removed.UnsortedList(), model.XdsLogDetails{}, true, nil
86: }
87: 
88: func appendAddress(addr model.AddressInfo, requestedType string, aliases []string, have sets.Set[string], resources model.Resources) model.Resources {
89: 	n := addr.ResourceName()
90: 	have.Insert(n)
91: 	switch requestedType {
92: 	case v3.WorkloadType:
93: 		if addr.GetWorkload() != nil {
94: 			resources = append(resources, &discovery.Resource{
95: 				Name:     n,
96: 				Aliases:  aliases,
97: 				Resource: protoconv.MessageToAny(addr.GetWorkload()), // TODO: pre-marshal
98: 			})
99: 		}
100: 	case v3.AddressType:

36: // GenerateDeltas computes Workload resources. This is design to be highly optimized to delta updates,
37: // and supports *on-demand* client usage. A client can subscribe with a wildcard subscription and get all
38: // resources (with delta updates), or on-demand and only get responses for specifically subscribed resources.
39: //
40: // Incoming requests may be for VIP or Pod IP addresses. However, all responses are Workload resources, which are pod based.
41: // This means subscribing to a VIP may end up pushing many resources of different name than the request.
42: // On-demand clients are expected to handle this (for wildcard, this is not applicable, as they don't specify any resources at all).
43: func (e WorkloadGenerator) GenerateDeltas(
44: 	proxy *model.Proxy,
45: 	req *model.PushRequest,
46: 	w *model.WatchedResource,
47: ) (model.Resources, model.DeletedResources, model.XdsLogDetails, bool, error) {
48: 	addresses := req.AddressesUpdated
49: 	isReq := req.IsRequest()
50: 	if !isReq && len(addresses) == 0 {
51: 		// Nothing changed...
52: 		return nil, nil, model.XdsLogDetails{}, false, nil
53: 	}
54: 
55: 	if !w.Wildcard {
56: 		return e.generateDeltasOndemand(proxy, req, w)
57: 	}
58: 
59: 	reqAddresses := addresses
60: 	if isReq {
61: 		reqAddresses = nil
62: 	}
63: 	addrs, removed := e.Server.Env.ServiceDiscovery.AddressInformation(reqAddresses)
64: 	// Note: while "removed" is a weird name for a resource that never existed, this is how the spec works:
65: 	// https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/api-docs/xds_protocol#id2
66: 	have := sets.New[string]()
67: 	resources := make(model.Resources, 0, len(addrs))
68: 	for _, addr := range addrs {
69: 		resources = appendAddress(addr, w.TypeUrl, nil, have, resources)
70: 	}
71: 
72: 	if isReq {
73: 		// If it's a full push, AddressInformation won't have info to compute the full set of removals.
74: 		// Instead, we need can see what resources are missing that we were subscribe to; those were removed.
75: 		// During a request, a client will send a subscription request with its current state, so our removals will be:
76: 		// ZtunnelCurrentResources - IstiodCurrentResources (+ empty `removed`).
77: 		removed = req.Delta.Subscribed.Difference(have).Merge(removed)
78: 	}
79: 
80: 	// Due to the high resource count in WDS at scale, we elide updating ResourceNames here.
81: 	// A name will be ~50-100 bytes. So 50k pods would be 5MB per XDS client (plus overheads around GC, sets, fragmentation, etc).
82: 	// Fortunately, we do not actually need this: the only time we need to know the state in Ztunnel is on reconnection which we handle from
83: 	// `req.Delta.Subscribed`.

85: 	return resources, removed.UnsortedList(), model.XdsLogDetails{}, true, nil
86: }

88: func appendAddress(addr model.AddressInfo, requestedType string, aliases []string, have sets.Set[string], resources model.Resources) model.Resources {
89: 	n := addr.ResourceName()
90: 	have.Insert(n)
91: 	switch requestedType {
92: 	case v3.WorkloadType:
93: 		if addr.GetWorkload() != nil {
94: 			resources = append(resources, &discovery.Resource{
95: 				Name:     n,
96: 				Aliases:  aliases,
97: 				Resource: protoconv.MessageToAny(addr.GetWorkload()), // TODO: pre-marshal
98: 			})
99: 		}
100: 	case v3.AddressType:
101: 		proto := addr.Marshaled
102: 		if proto == nil {
103: 			proto = protoconv.MessageToAny(addr)
104: 		}
105: 
106: 		resources = append(resources, &discovery.Resource{
107: 			Name:     n,
108: 			Aliases:  aliases,
109: 			Resource: proto,
110: 		})
111: 	}
112: 	return resources
113: }

115: func (e WorkloadGenerator) Generate(proxy *model.Proxy, w *model.WatchedResource, req *model.PushRequest) (model.Resources, model.XdsLogDetails, error) {
116: 	resources, _, details, _, err := e.GenerateDeltas(proxy, req, w)
117: 	return resources, details, err
118: }

120: func (e WorkloadGenerator) generateDeltasOndemand(
121: 	proxy *model.Proxy,
122: 	req *model.PushRequest,
123: 	w *model.WatchedResource,
124: ) (model.Resources, model.DeletedResources, model.XdsLogDetails, bool, error) {
125: 	isReq := req.IsRequest()
126: 	subs := w.ResourceNames
127: 
128: 	var addresses sets.String
129: 	if isReq {
130: 		// this is from request, we only send response for the subscribed address
131: 		// At t0, a client request A, we only send A and additional resources back to the client.
132: 		// At t1, a client request B, we only send B and additional resources back to the client, no A here.
133: 		addresses = req.Delta.Subscribed
134: 	} else {
135: 		// this is from the external triggers instead of request
136: 		// send response for all the subscribed intersect with the updated
137: 		addresses = req.AddressesUpdated.Intersection(subs)
138: 	}
139: 
140: 	// We only need this for on-demand. This allows us to subscribe the client to resources they
141: 	// didn't explicitly request.
142: 	// For wildcard, they subscribe to everything already.
143: 	additional := e.Server.Env.ServiceDiscovery.AdditionalPodSubscriptions(proxy, addresses, subs)
144: 	if addresses == nil {
145: 		addresses = additional
146: 	} else {
147: 		addresses.Merge(additional)
148: 	}
149: 
150: 	if len(addresses) == 0 {
151: 		if isReq {
152: 			// We need to respond for requests, even if we have nothing to respond with
153: 			return make(model.Resources, 0), nil, model.XdsLogDetails{}, false, nil
154: 		}
155: 		// For NOP pushes, no need
156: 		return nil, nil, model.XdsLogDetails{}, false, nil
157: 	}
158: 	addrs, removed := e.Server.Env.ServiceDiscovery.AddressInformation(addresses)
159: 	// Note: while "removed" is a weird name for a resource that never existed, this is how the spec works:
160: 	// https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/api-docs/xds_protocol#id2
161: 	have := sets.New[string]()
162: 	resources := make(model.Resources, 0, len(addrs))
163: 	for _, addr := range addrs {
164: 		aliases := addr.Aliases()
165: 		removed.DeleteAll(aliases...)
166: 		resources = appendAddress(addr, w.TypeUrl, aliases, have, resources)
167: 	}
168: 
169: 	proxy.Lock()
170: 	defer proxy.Unlock()
171: 	// For on-demand, we may have requested a VIP but gotten Pod IPs back. We need to update
172: 	// the internal book-keeping to subscribe to the Pods, so that we push updates to those Pods.
173: 	w.ResourceNames = subs.Merge(have)
174: 	return resources, removed.UnsortedList(), model.XdsLogDetails{}, true, nil
175: }

177: type WorkloadRBACGenerator struct {
178: 	Server *DiscoveryServer
179: }
180: 
181: func (e WorkloadRBACGenerator) GenerateDeltas(
182: 	proxy *model.Proxy,
183: 	req *model.PushRequest,
184: 	w *model.WatchedResource,
185: ) (model.Resources, model.DeletedResources, model.XdsLogDetails, bool, error) {
186: 	var updatedPolicies sets.Set[model.ConfigKey]
187: 	expected := sets.New[string]()
188: 	if req.Forced {
189: 		// Full update, expect everything
190: 		expected.Merge(w.ResourceNames)
191: 	} else {
192: 		// The ambient store will send all of these as kind.AuthorizationPolicy, even if generated from PeerAuthentication,
193: 		// so we can only fetch these ones.
194: 		updatedPolicies = model.ConfigsOfKind(req.ConfigsUpdated, kind.AuthorizationPolicy)
195: 
196: 		if len(updatedPolicies) == 0 {
197: 			// This was a incremental push for a resource we don't watch... skip
198: 			return nil, nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, false, nil
199: 		}
200: 
201: 		for k := range updatedPolicies {
202: 			expected.Insert(k.Namespace + "/" + k.Name)
203: 		}
204: 	}
205: 
206: 	resources := make(model.Resources, 0)
207: 	policies := e.Server.Env.ServiceDiscovery.Policies(updatedPolicies)
208: 	for _, p := range policies {
209: 		n := p.ResourceName()
210: 		expected.Delete(n) // delete the generated policy name, left the removed ones
211: 		resources = append(resources, &discovery.Resource{
212: 			Name:     n,
213: 			Resource: protoconv.MessageToAny(p.Authorization),
214: 		})
215: 	}
216: 
217: 	return resources, sets.SortedList(expected), model.XdsLogDetails{}, true, nil
218: }
219: 
220: func (e WorkloadRBACGenerator) Generate(proxy *model.Proxy, w *model.WatchedResource, req *model.PushRequest) (model.Resources, model.XdsLogDetails, error) {
221: 	resources, _, details, _, err := e.GenerateDeltas(proxy, req, w)
222: 	return resources, details, err
223: }
